authors: Pike, R.J.; Evans, I.S.; Hengl, T.
title: Geomorphometry: A Brief Guide
container_title: Geomorphometry: Concepts, Software, Applications (Developments in Soil Science 33)
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Defines geomorphometry as quantitative terrain analysis and supports the general scientific framing of raster-derived terrain measures.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: General geomorphometric context only; it does not establish marine data provenance, an algorithmic implementation, or an appropriate scale for this package.

authors: Horn, B.K.P.
title: Hill shading and the reflectance map
authoritative_verification_source: IEEE DOI record: https://doi.org/10.1109/PROC.1981.11918; terra terrain documentation: https://rspatial.github.io/terra/reference/terrain.html
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Gives numerical-method provenance for the eight-neighbour slope and aspect option called by terra in the stated configuration.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Supports method lineage only. It does not independently validate blueterra or define its local Laplacian-style index.

authors: Zevenbergen, L.W.; Thorne, C.R.
title: Quantitative analysis of land surface topography
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Provides established terrain-analysis terminology for slope, aspect, and curvature families in the Introduction and Discussion.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Do not use it to call blueterra's unscaled four-neighbour Laplacian-style index plan, profile, mean, or Gaussian curvature.

authors: Minár, J.; Evans, I.S.; Jenčo, M.
title: A comprehensive system of definitions of land surface (topographic) curvatures, with implications for their application in geoscience modelling and prediction
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports the need to distinguish named geometric curvatures from the manuscript's four-neighbour Laplacian-style index.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Terminological/theoretical context only; it is not a reference implementation for the package's index.

authors: Riley, S.J.; DeGloria, S.D.; Elliot, R.
title: A terrain ruggedness index that quantifies topographic heterogeneity
authoritative_verification_source: Montana State University Library journal archive: https://arc.lib.montana.edu/ojs/index.php/IJS/article/view/2914
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Provides the original TRI-family context and an eight-neighbour grid-cell formulation.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: No DOI is assigned in the journal archive. The citation does not mean that every terra TRI option or blueterra output is numerically identical to the original formulation.

authors: De Reu, J.; Bourgeois, J.; Bats, M.; Zwertvaegher, A.; Gelorini, V.; De Smedt, P.; Chu, W.; Antrop, M.; De Maeyer, P.; Finke, P.; Van Meirvenne, M.; Verniers, J.; Crombé, P.
title: Application of the topographic position index to heterogeneous landscapes
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports defining TPI/BPI as focal elevation minus a neighbourhood mean and motivates scale-specific interpretation.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Terrestrial methodological context only; it does not establish marine ecological prediction or a universal BPI window.

authors: Sappington, J.M.; Longshore, K.M.; Thompson, D.B.
title: Quantifying landscape ruggedness for animal habitat analysis: a case study using bighorn sheep in the Mojave Desert
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Provides the methodological antecedent for vector ruggedness measure (VRM), based on dispersion of surface-normal vectors.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: The manuscript must call its local product VRM-style unless it verifies a byte-for-byte reproduction of a named external implementation.

authors: Jenness, J.S.
title: Calculating landscape surface area from digital elevation models
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Provides context for raster-derived surface-area and surface-to-planar-area ratio measures.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Jenness describes a triangulation-based method. blueterra's 1/cos(slope) ratio is an approximation and must not be presented as Jenness's triangulated measurement.

authors: Grohmann, C.H.; Smith, M.J.; Riccomini, C.
title: Multiscale analysis of topographic surface roughness in the Midland Valley, Scotland
container_title: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
authoritative_verification_source: IEEE DOI record: https://doi.org/10.1109/TGRS.2010.2053546; institutional publication record: https://researchinnovation.kingston.ac.uk/en/publications/multiscale-analysis-of-topographic-surface-roughness-in-the-midla-2
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports the point that roughness measures and their behaviour depend on definition, window, and data resolution.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Terrestrial example; use only for metric/scale interpretation, not a claim about seabed processes or package performance.

authors: Hengl, T.
title: Finding the right pixel size
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports treating raster cell size as an analytical decision that changes terrain representation and derivative support.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Does not prescribe a universal bathymetric resolution or validate any particular blueterra setting.

authors: Thompson, J.A.; Bell, J.C.; Butler, C.A.
title: Digital elevation model resolution: effects on terrain attribute calculation and quantitative soil-landscape modeling
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports reporting resolution sensitivity because derived terrain attributes change with DEM resolution.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Terrestrial study only; it does not establish a justified smoothing window or focal size for the example bathymetry.

authors: Zhang, W.; Montgomery, D.R.
title: Digital elevation model grid size, landscape representation, and hydrologic simulations
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports the general proposition that grid size affects terrain representation.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Hydrologic context only; do not apply it as evidence for a marine-process claim.

authors: Lecours, V.; Dolan, M.F.J.; Micallef, A.; Lucieer, V.L.
title: A review of marine geomorphometry, the quantitative study of the seafloor
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Provides primary marine-geomorphometry framing and identifies marine data collection, uncertainty, scale, and validation as important considerations.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Supports methodological framing and limitations, not ecological causality or a single preferred scale.

authors: Dolan, M.F.J.; Lucieer, V.L.
title: Variation and uncertainty in bathymetric slope calculations using geographic information systems
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports discussing that slope results can vary with algorithms, grid resolution, analysis scale, and uncertainty in input bathymetry.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: It does not validate a particular implementation or provide an uncertainty estimate for the undocumented bundled examples.

authors: Lecours, V.; Devillers, R.; Lucieer, V.L.; Brown, C.J.
title: Artefacts in marine digital terrain models: a multiscale analysis of their impact on the derivation of terrain attributes
container_title: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports the limitation that bathymetric artefacts can propagate into terrain attributes and that effects can be scale dependent.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: This is a caution against unverified input quality; it is not evidence that blueterra detects, corrects, or quantifies artefacts.

authors: Misiuk, B.; Lecours, V.; Dolan, M.F.J.; Robert, K.
title: Evaluating the suitability of multi-scale terrain attribute calculation approaches for seabed mapping applications
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports separating grid-resolution, preprocessing, and focal-neighbourhood effects and reporting scale in transferable map units as well as cells.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Does not select a universally correct scale or validate a terrain-only ecological interpretation.

authors: Misiuk, B.; Lecours, V.; Bell, T.
title: A multiscale approach to mapping seabed sediments
authoritative_verification_source: PLOS publisher record: https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0193647; PubMed Central record: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5831638/
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports the rationale for evaluating seabed terrain attributes at multiple stated scales.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Used for scale-context only; it does not allow blueterra terrain metrics alone to be presented as sediment predictions.

authors: Wilson, M.F.J.; O'Connell, B.; Brown, C.; Guinan, J.C.; Grehan, A.J.
title: Multiscale terrain analysis of multibeam bathymetry data for habitat mapping on the continental slope
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports marine multiscale terrain-analysis context, including BPI as a relative-position measure.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Supports scale-sensitivity motivation, not a universal neighbourhood size, a habitat class, or physical-process prediction.

authors: Lundblad, E.R.; Wright, D.J.; Miller, J.; Larkin, E.M.; Rinehart, R.; Naar, D.F.; Donahue, B.T.; Anderson, S.M.; Battista, T.
title: A benthic terrain classification scheme for American Samoa
authoritative_verification_source: Taylor & Francis DOI record: https://doi.org/10.1080/01490410600738021; NOAA InPort cross-reference: https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/inport/item/12615
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Provides historic BPI, depth, slope, and multiscale benthic-terrain classification context.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Its thresholds and classification scheme are regional and application specific; do not use them as blueterra defaults.

authors: Erdey-Heydorn, M.D.
title: An ArcGIS seabed characterization toolbox developed for investigating benthic habitats
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Provides comparable ArcGIS seabed-characterization and BPI workflow context for the Discussion.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Comparison must be about scope and implementation transparency, not unsupported speed, accuracy, or ease-of-use claims.

authors: Walbridge, S.; Slocum, N.; Pobuda, M.; Wright, D.J.
title: Unified geomorphological analysis workflows with Benthic Terrain Modeler
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Provides Benthic Terrain Modeler context and supports describing transparent, documented bathymetric terrain-analysis workflows.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: It is not evidence that blueterra is numerically identical to Benthic Terrain Modeler or performs its classification functions.

authors: Ilich, A.R.; Misiuk, B.; Lecours, V.; Murawski, S.A.
title: MultiscaleDTM: an open-source R package for multiscale geomorphometric analysis
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Provides an R-based comparison point for multiscale geomorphometric calculation and the importance of stating focal support in map units.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Discuss as a complementary R package; do not claim that blueterra replaces it or compare performance without matched measurements.

authors: Lecours, V.; Devillers, R.; Simms, A.E.; Lucieer, V.L.; Brown, C.J.
title: Towards a framework for terrain attribute selection in environmental studies
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports organizing terrain attributes by conceptual groups and avoiding an unexamined inventory of correlated measures.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Supports selection framing only; it does not prescribe the package's metric catalog or establish a habitat-model result.

authors: Misiuk, B.; Brown, C.J.
title: Benthic habitat mapping: a review of three decades of mapping biological patterns on the seafloor
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports distinguishing terrain characterization from biological mapping, modelling, and validation.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Do not cite it as evidence that a terrain metric predicts habitat or ecology on its own.

authors: Sherman, C.; Nemeth, M.; Ruíz, H.; Bejarano, I.; Appeldoorn, R.; Pagán, F.; Schärer, M.; Weil, E.
title: Geomorphology and benthic cover of mesophotic coral ecosystems of the upper insular slope of southwest Puerto Rico
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports regional southwest-Puerto-Rico geomorphic and mesophotic-mapping context if the final documented example remains in that setting.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Do not infer present-day conditions, data provenance, or biological effects from this regional context citation.

authors: Appeldoorn, R.S.; Ballantine, D.L.; Carlo, M.; Cruz Motta, J.J.; Nemeth, M.; Ruiz, H.J.; Schizas, N.V.; Sherman, C.E.; Weil, E.; Yoshioka, P.M.
title: Intra-annual variation in mesophotic benthic assemblages on the insular slope of southwest Puerto Rico as a function of depth and geomorphology
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports a narrowly scoped regional setting sentence, if retained after example-data provenance is resolved.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Use only as regional/ecological context; it neither validates terrain metrics nor allows geomorphometry-only inference about assemblages.

authors: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Office of Coast Survey.
title: BlueTopo: National Bathymetric Source product documentation
authoritative_verification_source: NOAA specifications: https://nauticalcharts.noaa.gov/data/bluetopo_specs.html; FAQ: https://nauticalcharts.noaa.gov/data/bluetopo_faq.html; NOAA InPort metadata: https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/inport/item/75311
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports the Route-B data-product description, including its multi-source structure, uncertainty/contributors layers, use limitations, acknowledgement request, and product-level reuse context.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: The product documentation does not establish the historic bundled-example lineage. The formal data citation for final real-data figures is the exact dated Route-B tile record below, not this generic product page alone.

authors: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Office of Coast Survey.
title: BlueTopo elevation tile BH54S4ZB, dated 2025-11-17
pages_or_article: 4 m GeoTIFF; tile delivery 2025-12-19 14:56:59 UTC; accessed 2026-07-11 18:21:04 UTC
authoritative_verification_source: Official tile: https://noaa-ocs-nationalbathymetry-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/BlueTopo/BH54S4ZB/BlueTopo_BH54S4ZB_20251117.tiff; retained official tile scheme: https://noaa-ocs-nationalbathymetry-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/BlueTopo/_BlueTopo_Tile_Scheme/BlueTopo_Tile_Scheme_20260626_132625.gpkg; Route-B manifest: article/data_provenance/results/bluetopo_example_manifest.csv; product specifications: https://nauticalcharts.noaa.gov/data/bluetopo_specs.html
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Formal data citation for the new Route-B real-data maps, summaries, scale-sensitivity results, and reproducibility outputs. The documented source is tile BH54S4ZB, 4 m, EPSG:6348 NAD83(2011) / UTM zone 19N; tile-specific vertical metadata are MSL(GEOID12B) height and article scripts applied no vertical transformation.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: This exact dated tile, its RAT, and its tile-scheme record were downloaded and checksum-verified for Route B. The citation is not evidence about the older `inst/extdata` southwest-Puerto-Rico fixtures, which are not used in final real-data outputs. Cite the access date, official acknowledgement request, and data manifest with this record.

authors: International Hydrographic Organization.
title: IHO Standards for Hydrographic Surveys, S-44
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports the general statement that hydrographic survey quality and uncertainty need documented reference-system and quality context.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Do not present the undocumented bundled examples as S-44 compliant or use this standard as a substitute for tile-specific metadata.

authors: Pebesma, E.
title: Simple Features for R: standardized support for spatial vector data
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports the R simple-features vector data model used by optional polygon, transect, and corridor workflows.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Supports data-model context, not a claim that blueterra implements all sf algorithms.

authors: Hijmans, R.J.; Brown, A.; Barbosa, M.
title: terra: Spatial Data Analysis
authoritative_verification_source: Executed installed-package citation metadata on 2026-07-11; versioned CRAN archive: https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/Archive/terra/terra_1.9-27.tar.gz; terrain documentation: https://rspatial.github.io/terra/reference/terrain.html
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Documents the evaluated upstream package version and supports wording that direct comparisons are wrapper equivalence/upstream-call agreement.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: The evaluation used terra 1.9-27. Do not call comparisons with terra independent cross-implementation validation.

authors: Baston, D.
title: exactextractr: Fast Extraction from Raster Datasets using Polygons
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports the exact-intersection extraction operation used when exact=TRUE.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: It does not support calling blueterra summaries coverage-fraction weighted: package source excludes coverage_fraction before its summary calculations unless that behaviour is changed and re-evaluated.

authors: Wu, Q.; Brown, A.
title: whitebox: WhiteboxTools R Frontend
authoritative_verification_source: CRAN record: https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=whitebox; evaluated citation/packageVersion metadata on 2026-07-11
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports describing WhiteboxTools as a complementary terrain-analysis environment available through an R frontend.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Do not make speed, accuracy, or completeness comparisons without matched algorithms, inputs, settings, outputs, and measurements.

authors: Lindsay, J.B.
title: Whitebox GAT: a case study in geomorphometric analysis
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Provides peer-reviewed geomorphometric context for the Whitebox GAT predecessor in the Discussion.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: It is not a citation for the currently installed WhiteboxTools binary and does not establish equivalence with blueterra.

authors: Marwick, B.; Boettiger, C.; Mullen, L.
title: Packaging data analytical work reproducibly using R (and friends)
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Supports the reproducibility rationale for a versioned analysis compendium, execution scripts, environments, results, and manifests.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Supports research-compendium practice; reproducibility claims in the article must still be limited to the documented clean-environment reruns.

authors: R Core Team.
title: R: A Language and Environment for Statistical Computing
authoritative_verification_source: Official R Project: https://www.R-project.org/; executed R.version.string recorded on 2026-07-11
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Documents the R runtime used to execute analyses, figures, and tests.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: Identifies the executed runtime only; it is not evidence of cross-platform support beyond documented test environments.

authors: Cordero, E.
title: blueterra: an R workflow for geomorphometric analysis of submerged terrain
authoritative_verification_source: GitHub release v0.2.0 and immutable annotated tag verified on 2026-07-11
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Provides the versioned software citation in Availability and Requirements.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: The release is a public GitHub release and annotated tag. A persistent archival DOI has not been assigned and is not inferred from the release URL.

authors: Unresolved historic source record.
title: Legacy bundled southwest Puerto Rico example data
specific_manuscript_claim_scope: Records the non-citable provenance boundary for historic `inst/extdata` real-world rasters and sampling polygons, which are excluded from Route-B real-data analyses and figures.
scope_limit_or_discrepancy: The repository does not establish complete source-tile inventory, processing history, vertical datum, or vector reuse status for the historic fixtures. Do not cite them as NOAA BlueTopo or use them for final real-data results. This is not a bibliography entry.
